examples: 3RD ST & EGGLESTON AVE -> 3rd St & Eggleston Ave
3RD ST EAST OF GEST ST -> 3rd St East Of Gest St
3RD ST & GEST ST -> 3rd St & Gest St
fixes.label: Recased shouting stop names

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Rename stops to mixed case (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave').
findings.what: About 3302 stop names are written in ALL CAPS.
findings.why: Mixed-case names are easier to read in apps and are read more naturally by screen readers.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (1% marked accessible, 99% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_same_coordinates' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Equal shape distance same coordinates (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
findings.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
findings.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

findings.effort: A field at a time; not urgent.
findings.fix: Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
findings.what: Some files are missing recommended (not required) fields.
findings.why: Recommended fields like agency_phone or stop descriptions make the feed more useful to riders and trip planners.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_diff_coordinates_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Equal shape distance diff coordinates distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unknown_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unknown file (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 6 kinds of issue across 11892 instances (0 error, 11891 warning, 1 informational).

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 45 days.

findings.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
findings.what: 2 of 126 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
findings.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Sampled 9 times: 3 of 3 feeds healthy; 98.4% of scheduled trips had live predictions; 98.7% of vehicles on their route; predictions ran a median of 0s behind schedule.

criteria.detail: Service data covers the next 46 days.
States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 100% of trips.

conformance.summary: This feed earns the conformance mark: valid, current, and stating wheelchair access on nearly every stop and trip.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Your feed uses agency_id METRO; the NTD asks for your five-digit NTD ID (50012) so the feed lines up with your record.
ntd_id_alignment.fix: Change the agency_id to 50012 in agency.txt and update the matching agency_id in routes.txt.

pillars.detail: Service data covers the next 46 days.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed looks ready for your NTD certification: published at a public URL, valid, and current. The official check is still your D-10 certification.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 21 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (1, 16, 19, 20, 21, and more).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 3363 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("3RD ST 508", "3RD ST 640", "3rd ST. WEST OF BRENT SPENCE BRIDGE", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.
